title: Smart merge system — description-based deduplication + git safety
summary: Governance layer to prevent duplicate file creation during sync, with git safety checkpoints.
details: Two new scripts: scan_descriptions.py extracts frontmatter descriptions from vault folders; git_commit.py commits file state before any modification
New required frontmatter field 'description' on all entity types — serves as search key for merge decisions
Agent decision flow: scan folder → read descriptions → decide merge or create → git commit if modifying → notify user on conflicts
Scope rules per entity type: vision/strategy = aggressive merge, ideas/learnings = moderate, patterns = conservative, projects/people = no scan
Setup protocol now requires git as prerequisite and initializes git repo in vault during setup

title: Knowledge transfer capture protocol
summary: New protocol for capturing work knowledge transfer sessions into structured vault notes.
details: Triggered when user mentions KT, knowledge transfer, or handoff
Produces two documents per KT: structured summary in Work/KT/[topic].md and raw transcript in Work/KT/Transcripts/[topic].md
Extracts participants, technical details, decisions, gotchas, action items, unclear areas, and references
Both documents follow sync protocol (buffered, not immediate)

title: Daily log append fix — multi-sync deduplication
summary: Fixed critical bug where multiple syncs per day created separate daily log files instead of appending.
details: Sync protocol Step 4a now checks if Log/Daily/YYYY-MM-DD.md exists before creating
If exists: appends with timestamp header. If not: creates as before

title: Batch sync architecture — 3-layer memory system
summary: Major architectural overhaul replacing immediate Obsidian sync with a 3-layer memory system. ~90% fewer file operations during conversation.
details: Layer 1 (Active Memory, ~2000 tokens): insights.md, goals.md, hot-memory.md — read every session
Layer 2 (Brain Dump Buffer): write-only append log during conversation — zero token cost, never read until sync
Layer 3 (Obsidian Vault): detailed entities with full YAML frontmatter and wiki links, created during batch sync
New sync-protocol.md governs the 11-step batch process: validate → load state → process buffer → create/update entities → recalculate memory → archive buffer → confirm
First sync test: processed 4 brain dumps, created 11 entities, updated 2 existing, established 12 cross-links

title: New memory documents: insights.md and goals.md
summary: Two new active memory documents providing richer context than previous hot-memory alone.
details: insights.md (~700 tokens): live inference about the user — patterns, energy state, strategies working/not working, mindset, vision alignment
goals.md (~500 tokens): actionable priorities derived from insights — lead domino, top 3, what to avoid, health priority, emotional check, weekly/quarterly goals
hot-memory.md slimmed from 800 to 500 tokens by moving patterns/lifestyle/mindset to insights.md
Total active memory stays under 2000 tokens while providing richer context

title: Sync buffer and archive system
summary: Implemented sync-buffer.md as a detailed append-only brain dump log with archive-on-sync.
details: Every brain dump captured with full context: emotional state, names, numbers, quotes, reasoning, decisions
Extracted entities typed: DAILY_LOG, PROJECT_UPDATE, PERSON_UPDATE, PATTERN_UPDATE, TASK_NEW, IDEA, LEARNING
Buffer is NEVER read during conversation (zero token cost) — only consumed during sync
After successful sync, buffer archived to sync-archive/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS.md and cleared
Sync triggers: user explicit, cron interval (default 15 min), end of check-in, or buffer overflow

title: Inline wikilink requirement for knowledge graph structure
summary: Updated linking protocol to require inline wikilinks throughout note body, not just frontmatter.
details: Every entity mention (projects, people, patterns, ideas, learnings) must be linked on first reference
Added mandatory 'Related' section at bottom of all vault notes with 3-5 curated connections
Inline links create semantic relationships visible in Obsidian graph view — without them, graph shows isolated nodes

title: Workspace-root config and multi-vault support
summary: Session start protocol updated to support multiple vaults via workspace-level config separation.
details: Checks workspace root for .memspren-config (vault path pointer) before loading vault-internal files
File location table clarified to distinguish workspace root, vault skill files, and vault content

title: Documentation site launched
summary: Static documentation site with Getting Started guide, sidebar navigation, and GitHub integration.
details: Step-by-step setup instructions for both Claude and OpenClaw plugins
Left sidebar navigation with hash-based SPA routing

title: Netlify deployment configured
summary: Set up netlify.toml with production-only deployment rules. Only main branch changes trigger deploys.

title: OpenClaw skill v0.01 shipped
summary: First working version of memspren as an OpenClaw skill with session protocol and vault config loading.
details: Session start protocol with .memspren-config at workspace root
Vault-specific config loading and hot-memory context retrieval
Foundation for Zettelkasten-based knowledge graph management via obsidian-cli

title: Platform pivot from Claude to OpenClaw
summary: Strategic decision to evolve memspren from Claude plugin to OpenClaw skill.
details: Rationale: OpenClaw provides phone accessibility (Telegram/WhatsApp), better user management, and foundation for multi-agent architecture
v0.01 targets exploratory foundation; v1.0 targets production-ready custom agentic system

title: Initial concept and dual-audience publication
summary: Memspren concept introduced via simultaneous technical and non-technical articles.
details: Technical architecture on dev.to and non-technical motivation on Substack
Established vision for Zettelkasten-powered personal knowledge graph with Claude as thinking partner

title: Initial repository created with Claude skill
summary: Project initialized with Managing Second Brain skill for Claude.
details: Established foundation for Zettelkasten-based knowledge graph system
Protocols for setup, check-in, entity management, and linking
